expectations.text: Model identifies at least 2 vague terms from: robust, scalable, fast, gracefully
expectations.evidence: Problem section explicitly flags: 'The request uses vague quality terms ("robust", "scalable", "fast", "handle errors gracefully"). To make this implementable, these must be converted to measurable targets.' All 4 vague terms identified.

expectations.text: Model does NOT produce a spec where vague terms appear unquantified in prose
expectations.evidence: Every instance of vague language is accompanied by a [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] marker demanding metrics. Example: '[NEEDS CLARIFICATION: Define measurable SLOs replacing vague input: (a) p95 charge-attempt latency target, (b) max billing-cycle duration target, (c) target user volume/concurrency.]' No vague term appears as an accepted requirement.

expectations.text: Vague terms are replaced with [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] markers that demand specific metrics
expectations.evidence: Multiple NEEDS CLARIFICATION markers: business impact clarification, retry policy targets, and SLO definitions with specific metric categories (p95 latency, max cycle duration, user volume). The markers demand concrete numbers, not just acknowledgment.

expectations.text: Model asks clarifying questions before or during spec generation about what robust, scalable, etc. mean concretely
expectations.evidence: probing_questions.md contains 8 questions across 2 rounds (3 Critical, 3 High, 2 Medium). Critical question #3 directly addresses 'scalable': 'What concrete capacity target should we design for (users per cycle, max cycle duration, allowed Stripe API QPS)?'
